name: PR_REVIEW_LEARNINGS.md
purpose: Structured retro log of PR review rounds — patterns found, items fixed/rejected, key learnings — across PR #3 (R1+R2) and PR #5 (R1).
notes: Contains 3 review sessions. Each section: source tools, item counts by severity, pattern analysis, resolutions, pending user actions, key learnings. Learnings about atomic writes, helper-bypass, cross-round dedup, and false-positive validation are transferable with light sanitization (strip PR-specific line refs). Three categories of learnings: (1) port hygiene patterns (copy-as-is inherits upstream bugs), (2) reviewer behavior (static tools cannot see runtime guards, stale snapshots), (3) methodology (in-session vs agent dispatch decision rule, convergence-loop signal). Flagged sanitize-then-portable because methodology learnings would benefit any SoNash-descendant project's /pr-review skill.

name: TODOS.md
purpose: Human-readable rendered view of the active /todo backlog — auto-generated from todos.jsonl by scripts/planning/render-todos.js; do not edit directly.
notes: 16 active todos (0 P0, 7 P2, 9 P3), 5 completed. Active set dominated by #foundation-deferral and #sync-mechanism tags. Key clusters: Layer 2/3/4 gated items (T18/T19/T20/T21), sync-mechanism research dependencies (T2/T4/T9/T13), husky enrichment composites (T11/T12). Completed items confirm Foundation firm layers shipped (T1, T6, T14, T15, T16). Not portable — content is project-specific milestone backlog.

name: todos.jsonl
purpose: Machine-readable active todo backlog — canonical source of truth for /todo skill; one JSON object per task with full schema including id, title, description, priority, status, tags, context (branch + files), and timestamps.
notes: 22 lines = 22 records (21 shown in TODOS.md rendering + T21 added late). Schema: {id, title, description, priority, status, progress, tags, context:{branch, files}, createdAt, updatedAt, completedAt}. Priority distribution: 1xP1 (completed), 8xP2, 12xP3, 1xP3 completed. Status: 17 pending, 5 completed. Tags used as cross-cutting labels (#foundation-deferral, #sync-mechanism, #layer-2/3/4, #d19-skipped, #d34-reapproval-required). Context.files tracks affected files per task — useful for dependency tracing. Not portable — backlog is project-specific.

name: jason-os/
purpose: Research roadmap planning session for the 16-domain JASON-OS research program — decisions, domain definitions, dependency graph, status tracker, and cross-domain synthesis scaffold.
notes: 6 files: PLAN.md (22109), DECISIONS.md (12218), DEPENDENCIES.md (2930), RESEARCH_ROADMAP.md (2595), SYNTHESIS.md (1423), BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md (5232). PLAN.md defines 16 research domains in 3 tiers with 4-phase per-domain protocol (A:scope, B:execute, C:gate, D:reassess). DECISIONS.md logs 35 decisions covering domain definitions, structure, sequencing. RESEARCH_ROADMAP.md is a living tracker — current state: Domain 02a ongoing, all others not started. SYNTHESIS.md and DEPENDENCIES.md are scaffolded but empty pending first domain completion. BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md lists initial skills/infra deliberately not ported (session-end, pr-review, pr-retro, ci.yml, agents). This planning session pre-dates the Foundation implementation — it is the JASON-OS research architecture design. Not portable.

name: jason-os/PLAN.md
purpose: Master research roadmap — 16 domain definitions, 3-tier sequencing, per-domain protocol, orchestration artifact specs, and 7 execution steps for the JASON-OS research program.
notes: Domains 01-16 defined with purpose, scope, prior art, estimated scope, and dependency arrows. Domain 07 (Sync & Distribution) flagged as #1 priority. Domain 02a (Adoption Scouting) is ongoing/non-terminal. Three reassessment checkpoints after each tier. Orchestration artifacts: RESEARCH_ROADMAP.md (living tracker), SYNTHESIS.md (rolling cross-domain), DEPENDENCIES.md (living dep map). Step 7 routes to /deep-plan for implementation. References: brainstorm explored ~47 portable skills, ~16 agents, ~5 hooks.

name: jason-os/BOOTSTRAP_DEFERRED.md
purpose: Catalogue of items intentionally not ported during initial JASON-OS bootstrap — each with why-deferred rationale, revisit trigger, and source path in SoNash.
notes: Deferred skills: session-end (coupling to SoNash npm scripts, TDMS, reviews system), pr-review (71 sanitization hits, TDMS/Gemini/CodeRabbit coupling), pr-retro (coupling to pr-ecosystem-audit, TDMS, convergence-loop deliverable). Deferred infra: ci.yml (SoNash npm scripts + Firebase env vars), copilot-instructions.md (29 SoNash refs), custom statusline (Go binary with sonash-specific config), agents dir (stack-specific), GSD/TDMS/audit ecosystem (intentional skip). Each entry has a revisit trigger condition — surfacing dependency structure useful for schema design.

name: jason-os-mvp/
purpose: Foundation implementation planning session — full deep-plan output covering 6 layers of porting work from SoNash to JASON-OS, with per-file port analysis ledger, 38 decisions, diagnosis, handoff, and resume pointer.
notes: 6 files: PORT_ANALYSIS.md (53347 — largest; 33+ rows, one per ported file), PLAN.md (43057 — full layer-by-layer impl plan), DECISIONS.md (23250 — 38 decisions + 6 meta-instructions), DIAGNOSIS.md (15391 — initial state assessment), RESUME.md (4662 — session bookmark), HANDOFF.md (5032 — execution order + user-action preview). Status: Foundation firm layers COMPLETE (PR #3 merged), PR #5 merged. D19 gate closed with Layers 2/3/4 skipped (T18/T19/T20/T21 in /todo). Next: Step 6 /brainstorm sync-mechanism. PORT_ANALYSIS.md is the key sync-mechanism dependency — it tracks every ported file with verdicts (copy-as-is/sanitize-then-copy/redesign/skip).

name: jason-os-mvp/PLAN.md
purpose: Full implementation plan for JASON-OS Foundation — Layer 0+/0/1/2/3/4 port steps, pre-analysis templates, port-agent templates, MI/D cross-references, and audit checkpoints.
notes: Layer structure: 0+ (10 zero-cost wins, manual), 0 (2 ports), 1 (SESSION_CONTEXT + session-end + 3 hook wirings), Step 4 pre-push mini-phase (pr-review + pre-commit-fixer), Step 5 validation, Layers 2/3/4 gated. Extended sanitization regex embedded (sonash|SoNash|firebase|...). PORT_ANALYSIS.md row schema embedded (D13). Port-agent template embedded (D17). Meta-instructions MI-1 through MI-6 define protocol rules. References research findings G2/G4/G5/G6 from .research/jason-os-mvp/findings/.

name: jason-os-mvp/PORT_ANALYSIS.md
purpose: Central per-file port ledger — one row per file ported from SoNash, recording source path, target path, sanitization hits, upstream callers, downstream deps, verdict, port date, and commit SHA.
notes: Largest file in .planning/ (53KB). 33+ rows as of PR #3 backfill. Verdict distribution: mix of copy-as-is, sanitize-then-copy, redesign, skip, blocked-on-prereq. Contains extended regex, verdict legend, and row schema embedded for reference. This is the most structurally relevant artifact for sync-mechanism schema design — it directly models the file-registry concept (every file tracked with dependency and portability metadata). Its schema is a superset of what SKILL_INDEX.md 3.3 needs (T19 note: schema-reroll-risk). Key dependency for Piece 2 schema design.

name: jason-os-mvp/DECISIONS.md
purpose: Decision record for the Foundation implementation — 38 decisions + 6 meta-instructions covering port methodology, layer structure, agent dispatch, approval modes, PR-review setup, and deferred items.
notes: Decisions grouped by topic: port methodology (D13 schema, D17 agents, D21 regex), layer structure (D16 parallel, D18 per-commit, D34 staged approval), external reviews (D22/D23 Qodo+SonarCloud), post-Foundation routing (D14 MI-3 brainstorm). Meta-instructions MI-1 through MI-6 define protocol rules referenced throughout PLAN.md.

name: jason-os-mvp/DIAGNOSIS.md
purpose: Initial state assessment for Foundation planning — what exists in JASON-OS, what was ported from SoNash, gaps identified, strategic open questions, and caveats before implementation began.
notes: Consumed by PLAN.md and DECISIONS.md. Contains inventory of pre-Foundation state, per-skill port status, hook status, and research findings summary (from R-frpg/jason-os-mvp research). Not sampled in full — characterization based on file size and role in planning session.

name: jason-os-mvp/HANDOFF.md
purpose: Execution handoff document — ordered step list, user-action preview table, resume protocol, and invocation tracking notes for the Foundation implementation run.
notes: 15-step execution order table (firm + gated). User-action steps previewed: SoNash branch confirm, SonarCloud signup, Qodo install, npm install, gitleaks install, Step 5 validation session, per-layer re-approval. Resume protocol: read state file + /deep-plan skill recovery. Invocation tracking notes acknowledge write-invocation.ts is SoNash-only and stripped in 0c.

name: jason-os-mvp/RESUME.md
purpose: Session bookmark file — captures exact git state, what-is-done, what-is-next, outstanding user actions, and how to resume in next Claude Code session after PR #3 merge.
notes: Created end of Session 2. References PR #3 merge SHA (67b88f8), 33 PORT_ANALYSIS.md rows after Step 4 backfill, 16 active todos T1-T16. Next step: Step 5 validation session + D34 layer decisions. Outstanding: m1 SonarCloud PATH hotspots mark-as-safe. Pattern: RESUME.md is a reusable inter-session communication artifact — worth capturing in SoNash scan methodology.
